title: Educator field-test of printed Latin set + feedback
context: The project's quality bar is 'delivered, not merged': sheets must be accepted and used by a real educator, not merely rendered and committed. The educator field-test closes the last-mile loop for M1: an educator prints and uses the complete Latin set with real learners, returns structured feedback, and any confirmed adoption flips the relevant tasks from verifiedNeed:false to verifiedNeed:true. Defects found in the field become new tasks.
objective: Arrange for at least one educator to print and use the full Latin print set with real learners; collect structured feedback using a template; log results; open tasks for any defects; and update task verifiedNeed/requestor fields if adoption is confirmed.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 1 educator prints and uses the Latin sheet set (A4 or Letter, at least one template type, at least one accessibility variant) with real learners.
Structured feedback collected using a template covering: formation accuracy (does the sheet match how the educator teaches?), printability, accessibility variant utility, and overall usefulness.
Feedback logged in a committed document (e.g. docs/field-test-latin.md).
Any formation defects or accessibility issues identified become follow-up tasks.
If adoption is confirmed, affected tasks are updated: verifiedNeed:true and requestor set to the named educator/org.
resources: PLAN.md section 4 (Success metrics — educator adoption), section 8 (Definition of Shipped)
outreach-008 (partner contacts to recruit the field-test educator)
output: A committed field-test feedback log with at least one educator's structured response, any follow-up task IDs opened for defects, and verifiedNeed/requestor updates applied where adoption is confirmed.
